rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories From Jull Kinney And Further Responses To Pltf'S Form Interrogatories, Set One, From John Kinney And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 29, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs FRANK ANGELO ALIOTO and ROSEMARY ALIOTO, as Trustees of the ALIOTO FAMILY TRUST'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories From Jull Kinney And Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories, Set One, From John Kinney And Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff to appear as court has no papers. If motion is opposed, defendant to appear with proof of filed opposition, otherwise assuming moving papers are in order motion will be granted. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AMY OSBORN VS. EMILY HO KWAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To Amend X-Complaint To Add Cause Of Action For Statutory Indemnity And Contribution Pursuant To Ccp 473
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 29, 2013, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT PATRICK ADSUAR's Motion For Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Add Cause Of Action For Statutory Indemnity And Contribution Pursuant To CCP § 473. Granted. (CCP § 473(a).) There is a strong policy favoring liberality in amending pleadings. Further, the Court finds that the delay has not prejudiced CCSF, and can be ameliorated by a continuance of the trial date. Mr. Adsuar is granted leave to file and serve a First Amended Cross-Complaint within 5 days after notice of entry of order. The new allegations do not change new theories of recovery not raised in his tort claim; Mr. Adsuar specifically requested indemnity and contribution in his tort claim. (Delbon Decl., Exhibit B.) The trial date of December 2, 2013 is vacated and a new trial date is set for March 17, 2013. The new trial date will be treated as the initial trial date for all discovery and pre-trial statutory deadlines. Defendant's motion for summary judgment, now set for hearing on November 1, 2013, will be vacated in light of this ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Be Relieved Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 29, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff VICTORIA PETERSON'S Motion To Be Relieved Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. As plaintiff recognizes she is seeking reconsideration of prior orders. Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, upon which she relies, has a number of requirements that must be met. Plaintiff has not satisfied these requirements, including but not limited to bringing before the judge who issued the order within 10 days after service of the order upon the party. (Code of Civ. Proc. §1008(a).) Moreover, there is no authority that Code of Civil Procedure section 473 can be used to circumvent the requirements of section 1008. (Code of Civ. Proc. §1008(e).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LANCE FARRELL VS. RYSIGO TECHNOLOGIES CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rysigo Technologies Corporation Motion To Compel Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 29, 2013, Line 7, Defendant Rysigo Technologies Corporation Motion To Compel Deposition. The Court has reviewed all papers filed in support and opposition to the motions, including the supplemental briefs filed both parties on October 22, 2013. Since this matter was heard by the Pro Tem Judge on September 16, 2013, Plaintiff has attended his deposition. Thus, the motion to compel is now moot. As to sanctions, the Court finds that substantial justification did not exist for Plaintiff unilaterally continuing the deposition a few days before it was scheduled to occur on August 8 and 9, 2013. (CCP § 2025.450(g)(1).) Accordingly, sanctions in the amount of $2,822.90 will be awarded in favor of Rysigo Technologies Corporation and payable by Plaintiff Farrell within 30 days of entry of this order. This dollar amount represents defendants' attorney fees for bringing this motion. Sanctions against Plaintiff's counsel are denied because in the Notice of Motion, Rysigo Technologies did not explicitly request sanctions against counsel. "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party and attorney against whom the sanction is sought . ¿" (CCP § 2023.040.) The request for sanctions for the lost revenues of Mr. Maqsood, Chief Operating Officer of Rysigo Technologies Corporation is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEONID GONCHAROV et al VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 29, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Hearing required. Counsel are requested to address the following: 1. Why should the Court get involved in a novel dispute when the PUC is in the midst of addressing this issue? 2. Whether the nature of the relief requested by plaintiffs here is such that it does not implicate the concerns in Alvarado v. Selma Convalescent Hosp. (2007) 153 Cal. App. 4th 1294? 3. How do plaintiffs allege an existing relationship with "the passenger public" sufficient to support the 2nd and 3rd causes of action in light of Roth v. Rhodes (1994) 25 Cal. App. 4th 530, 546? 4. As a practical matter, if plaintiffs are able to state a claim for the 2nd and 3rd causes of action, what is the basis for the Court to abstain on the equitable causes of action? Does the adjudication of the 2nd and 3rd causes of action--if properly pleaded--necessarily implicate the same issues that Defendants seek the court to abstain from addressing? =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MCALEESE, PAUL (DBA 530 JACKSON STREET, INC.) VS. RICHARD T. FERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 29, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANTs RICHARD FERKO and RICHARD T. FERKO, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is overruled in part and sustained in part with leave to amend. Defendants' demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Plaintiff Paul McAleese. Plaintiff McAleese has not alleged facts that he is the real party of interest to sue on behalf of Plaintiff corporation 530 Jackson Street. (See Fladeboe v. American Isuzu Motors Inc. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 42, 55.) Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to allege facts, if Plaintiffs can do so in good faith, that an agreement for legal representation existed between Defendant Ferko and Plaintiff McAleese, as an individual. Defendants' demurrers on the ground that all causes of action are barred by the applicable statute of limitations is overruled. Plaintiff 530 Jackson Street, Inc. alleges that it discovered the wrongful conduct in May 2012; the original complaint was filed February 14, 2013, which is within 1 year. As such, the causes of action are not barred by CCP §§340.6, 337, or 338. Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action (fraud) and fourth cause of action (constructive fraud) is overruled; Plaintiffs have alleged facts with sufficient particularity to meet the heightened pleading standard for fraud. Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action for accounting is overruled as Plaintiffs have alleged that a fiduciary relationship existed and that Plaintiffs never received an accounting of the settlement money that Defendants collected. (FAC 10, 36; see Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 156, 179.) The Court does not consider the exhibits attached to Plaintiffs' opposition to demurrer in ruling on this demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 29, 2013, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANTs ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, PAULA BUTLER and WILLIAM HUTTON's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer filed by cross-defendants Alton Management Corporation, Paula Butler, and William Hutton (¿Alton Management Defendants¿) to the Cross-Complaint is off calendar, per the June 26, 2013 Order Granting Motion to Stay this action. The Requests for Judicial Notice are granted. The June 26, 2013 order stayed the entire action, including the cross-complaint. (Further, the Stipulation and Order to Stay Cross-Complaint Against Farmers Insurance Exchange and Truck Insurance Exchange, filed July 22, 2013, is consistent with the June 26, 2013 order.) The risk of inconsistent findings applies equally to the causes of action brought against the Alton Management Defendants, as the allegations in the Cross-Complaint arise from the same conduct complained of in the underlying action (CGC-11-509077). The Alton Management Defendants would also be prejudiced by having to fight litigation on two-fronts. The entire action is stayed, pending resolution of the underlying action or further order of this Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
